input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.RefreshCommand>
            <Binding Path="RefreshCommand" />
        </CollectionView.RefreshCommand>

        <!-- Wrap in a RefreshView for pull-to-refresh -->
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

However, `CollectionView` doesn't directly support pull-to-refresh — you wrap it in a **`RefreshView`**:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                    SelectionChangedCommandParameter="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;          // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;           // reset so re-selection works
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Property | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | One-way | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` → `SelectedItem` | **TwoWay** | Tracks selection; reset to `null` after navigation |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` → `ItemSelectedCommand` | One-way | Fires navigation logic |
| `IsRefreshing` → `IsRefreshing` | TwoWay | Controls the refresh spinner; set `false` when done |
| `RefreshView.Command` → `RefreshCommand` | One-way | Triggers API reload on pull |

**Important notes:**
- **`RefreshView` wraps `CollectionView`** — the refresh properties live on `RefreshView`, not `CollectionView`.
- **Set `SelectedItem = null`** after navigating, otherwise tapping the same item again won't fire `SelectionChanged`.
- **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** at the end of your refresh command to dismiss the spinner.
